atmega2560.name: Atmel ATmega2560
atmega2560.notes: Used by Arduino Mega 2560. No factory unique-ID register.

atmega328p.name: Atmel ATmega328P
atmega328p.notes: Used by Arduino Uno R3 and Arduino Nano. No factory unique-ID register; mcu_id is zero-filled on this family.

atmega32u4.name: Atmel ATmega32U4
atmega32u4.notes: Used by Arduino Leonardo and Micro. Native USB-CDC. No factory unique-ID.

atmega4809.name: Atmel ATmega4809 (megaAVR)
atmega4809.notes: Used by Arduino Nano Every. megaAVR core (different from classic AVR). Every GPIO supports edge interrupts.

esp32.name: Espressif ESP32 (classic)
esp32.notes:
Classic ESP32 (Tensilica Xtensa LX6 dual-core, WiFi+BLE).
ADC1 on GPIO 32-39 (always usable). ADC2 on GPIO 0,2,4,12-15,25-27 (conflicts with WiFi when WiFi is active —
conservatively excluded from analog_in declarations).
GPIO 6-11 are reserved for SPI flash (board-dependent — not user-accessible on most modules).
GPIO 34-39 are input-only (no DIGITAL_OUT capability).
PWM via LEDC peripheral works on any output-capable GPIO.
mcu_id source: ESP.getEfuseMac() (factory MAC, 48 bits).

esp32c3.name: Espressif ESP32-C3
esp32c3.notes:
ESP32-C3 (RISC-V single-core, WiFi+BLE 5.0).
ADC on GPIO 0-4 (5 channels total). One I2C peripheral.
No native USB OTG (USB Serial/JTAG only).

esp32s2.name: Espressif ESP32-S2
esp32s2.notes:
ESP32-S2 (Tensilica Xtensa LX7 single-core, WiFi only — no BLE).
ADC1 on GPIO 1-10, ADC2 on GPIO 11-20.
Native USB OTG. No DAC.

esp32s3.name: Espressif ESP32-S3
esp32s3.notes:
ESP32-S3 (Tensilica Xtensa LX7 dual-core, WiFi+BLE 5.0).
ADC1 on GPIO 1-10, ADC2 on GPIO 11-20.
Native USB OTG. Supports vector instructions for ML.

esp8266.name: Espressif ESP8266
esp8266.notes:
ESP8266 (Tensilica L106, WiFi only).
Single ADC pin (A0 = TOUT, GPIO17 internal). PWM is software (1kHz default).
GPIO numbering on most NodeMCU boards: D0=16, D1=5, D2=4, D3=0, D4=2, D5=14, D6=12, D7=13, D8=15.
GPIO 6-11 reserved for flash. mcu_id source: ESP.getChipId() (24-bit, padded).

imxrt1062.name: NXP i.MX RT1062
imxrt1062.notes:
i.MX RT1062 (Cortex-M7 @ 600 MHz, Teensy 4.0/4.1).
Crossbar ADC on selected GPIOs. Every pin can interrupt.
mcu_id source: HW_OCOTP_CFG0/CFG1 (NXP OCOTP fuses, 64-bit unique ID).

kinetis_k66.name: NXP Kinetis K66 (MK66FX1M0)
kinetis_k66.notes:
Kinetis K66 (Cortex-M4F @ 180 MHz, Teensy 3.6).
21 ADC channels. Every GPIO can interrupt.
mcu_id source: SIM_UIDH/MH/ML/L (Kinetis 128-bit unique-ID; we use upper 64 bits).

nrf52840.name: Nordic nRF52840
nrf52840.notes:
nRF52840 (Cortex-M4F, BLE 5.0 + Thread + Zigbee). 48 GPIOs across two ports (P0, P1).
6 ADC channels. Every GPIO supports interrupts via GPIOTE.
mcu_id source: NRF_FICR->DEVICEID[0..1] (64-bit factory ID).

rp2040.name: Raspberry Pi RP2040
rp2040.notes:
RP2040 has 30 GPIO, 4 ADC channels (GPIO 26-29; A0-A3), 8 PWM slices x 2 channels routable to any GPIO.
Every GPIO can serve as I2C SDA/SCL or SPI MOSI/MISO/SCK via the GPIO mux. Every GPIO supports interrupts.
mcu_id source is the external flash chip's JEDEC RUID via pico_get_unique_board_id().
